title: Bids & Info - Town of Kennard
headings: Town of Kennard – Request for Sealed Bids
snippet: Bids & Info - Town of Kennard Town of Kennard – Request for Sealed Bids The Town of Kennard is accepting sealed bids for Post Office and Town Hall maintenance work. Scope of Work Replace two interior light fixtures in the Kennard Post Office with LED fixtures using the same footp
content: bids & info - town of kennard town of kennard – request for sealed bids the town of kennard is accepting sealed bids for post office and town hall maintenance work. scope of work replace two interior light fixtures in the kennard post office with led fixtures using the same footprint so that repainting is not required. repair the post office door closer. repair two town hall door closers. the previous dusk-to-dawn light has been removed from the scope and is not part of this bid. viewing the work area anyone interested in bidding may visit the kennard post office between 12:30 pm and 4:00 pm, monday through friday, to view the area and understand the work involved. bid requirements bids must be sealed. the envelope must clearly state “usps repair bid” on the outside. bids may be hand delivered to the clerk or placed in the sewer payment drop box on the south side of town hall (black deposit box). bids must be received at least 48 hours before the next town council meeting. bids will be opened publicly during the town council meeting. the town of kennard reserves the right to reject any or all bids for any reason. location for submitting bids town of kennard 100 n. main street kennard, indiana 47351

title: Frequently Asked Questions - Town of Kennard
snippet: Frequently Asked Questions - Town of Kennard Quick Help Answers to the most common questions about living and doing business in Kennard, Indiana. How Kennard Works These FAQs are written specifically for how our small town operates — from wastewater payments and trash service to
content: frequently asked questions - town of kennard quick help answers to the most common questions about living and doing business in kennard, indiana. how kennard works these faqs are written specifically for how our small town operates — from wastewater payments and trash service to sheriff and volunteer fire department procedures. how do i pay my wastewater bill? wastewater payments can be made online using pay.gov. the bill includes one tote per account. pay wastewater online how do i get an additional trash tote? additional totes can be rented from bestway for an additional fee. contact best way of indiana directly at (765) 853-5714 or visit www.bestway-disposal.com . who do i call for animal issues? henry county animal control handles all animal-related concerns during business hours: (765) 529-8131. after hours, contact the henry county sheriff non-emergency line: (765) 521-7032. what are the speed limits in kennard? all town streets are 25 mph. state road 234 is 35 mph within town limits. please drive safely. when does the town council meet? the town board meets on the second wednesday of every month at 7:00 pm at town hall. agendas are posted on the minutes & agendas page. how do i contact the clerk/treasurer? clerk/treasurer jan lockridge phone: (765) 785-2331 text: (765) 524-0646 email: clerk@kennardin.com please contact during regular business hours. how do i submit a sealed bid? bids must be sealed and clearly marked on the envelope. they can be hand-delivered to the clerk or placed in the sewer payment drop box at town hall. full instructions are on the bids & info page. how do i contact the volunteer fire department? for general (non-emergency) questions: vfd@kennardin.com for all emergencies: dial 911

title: Fire Department - Town of Kennard
snippet: Fire Department - Town of Kennard Chief Ronald (Ron) Huffman Ronald (Ron) Huffman started his fire service career in 1985 as a volunteer firefighter with the Shirley Vol. Fire Department and has been with the Kennard Volunteer Fire Department since 1995. He was appointed Fire Chi
content: fire department - town of kennard chief ronald (ron) huffman ronald (ron) huffman started his fire service career in 1985 as a volunteer firefighter with the shirley vol. fire department and has been with the kennard volunteer fire department since 1995. he was appointed fire chief in 2019. ron has attended numerous fire training schools and is certified in many areas of fire service. ron is retired from the henry county emergency management. general fire department questions email: vfd@kennardin.com fire safety tips install and maintain smoke detectors create and practice a home escape plan keep flammable materials away from heat sources know how to use a fire extinguisher emergency preparedness for all emergencies: dial 911 volunteer fire department is ready to serve kennard and surrounding areas.

title: Home - Town of Kennard
headings: Kennard is located in western Henry County and is a part of Greensboro Township.
snippet: Home - Town of Kennard Current Weather in Kennard Loading weather... Kennard is located in western Henry County and is a part of Greensboro Township. Kennard, Indiana (ZIP 47351) is a small, friendly community with a rich history dating back to its platting in 1882. The town has
content: home - town of kennard current weather in kennard loading weather... kennard is located in western henry county and is a part of greensboro township. kennard, indiana (zip 47351) is a small, friendly community with a rich history dating back to its platting in 1882. the town has grown through the years and continues to see a housing boom. over the past ten years, three new parks have been developed. during the covid-19 pandemic, the town increased its medical volunteer personnel and purchased a newer vehicle to improve emergency medical assistance. watch town council meetings youtube channel public comments citizens are invited to address the town board during the public comment period at each meeting. meetings are held on the second wednesday of every month at 7:00 pm at town hall.

title: Kennard - Town of Kennard
snippet: Kennard - Town of Kennard Welcome to Kennard, Indiana Kennard is a small, friendly community located in western Henry County and part of Greensboro Township. The town was platted in 1882 and named after Jenkins Kennard. Founding of Kennard Kennard was officially platted on Septem
content: kennard - town of kennard welcome to kennard, indiana kennard is a small, friendly community located in western henry county and part of greensboro township. the town was platted in 1882 and named after jenkins kennard. founding of kennard kennard was officially platted on september 6, 1882, by surveyor daniel k. cook on land owned by cyrus c. hinshaw, john w. payne, w.m. dixon, charles hartley, and martha weaner. cyrus hinshaw served as the town's first postmaster and played a key role in its early development. the town grew as a railroad service point and agricultural community in greensboro township. verified from henry county historical society records. historic canning factory & copenhaver court kennard once had a large canning factory located next to the railroad tracks on the west side of town along west madison street. that site is now home to copenhaver court, where a basketball court was dedicated in honor of david copenhaver. view copenhaver court on google maps https://www.google.com/maps/search/copenhaver+court+kennard+in/@39.9039,-85.5194,3000m/data=!3m1!1e3 the 1974 tornado on april 3, 1974, during the super outbreak, an f4 tornado struck kennard and destroyed or damaged more than 70 percent of the town. the storm leveled homes and severely impacted the community, yet the town came together quickly to recover. emergency responders, the national guard, and the red cross provided vital assistance in the days that followed. we gratefully acknowledge the discover indiana project and the courier-times for documenting and sharing this important chapter of our town's history. read the full account here . aerial view of kennard view our town from above. see aerial view on google maps https://www.google.com/maps/@39.9039,-85.5194,2000m/data=!3m1!1e3 aerial imagery courtesy of google maps — thank you google for providing this clear view of our community. thinking of moving to kennard? explore current real estate listings in and around our town. view listings on mibor https://property.mibor.com/properties?combo_search=47351

title: Local Schools - Town of Kennard
snippet: Local Schools - Town of Kennard School District The Town of Kennard is within the Charles A. Beard Memorial School Corporation (approximately 8 miles from Kennard to Knightstown campus). Current Schools Serving Kennard Kennard students attend Knightstown Elementary, Knightstown I
content: local schools - town of kennard school district the town of kennard is within the charles a. beard memorial school corporation (approximately 8 miles from kennard to knightstown campus). current schools serving kennard kennard students attend knightstown elementary, knightstown intermediate, and knightstown high school. kennard high school consolidated with knightstown high school in 1957. by 1974 the town was served only by kennard elementary school (grades k-6). on april 3, 1974, an f4 tornado destroyed the original kennard elementary building. the school was rebuilt just north of the destroyed site and proudly continued to serve the community with distinction for nearly four decades. kennard elementary consistently achieved academic results above both district and state averages, earning the deep pride of many citizens and alumni who still fondly remember their time as kennard students. unfortunately, financial pressures within the charles a. beard memorial school corporation led to the closure of the beloved local school in 2013 as part of a larger consolidation effort. school bus stop: closest knightstown bus stop for kennard students is at the intersection of main street and state road 234. shirley has a bus stop for eastern. shenandoah has a bus stop north of town 1 mile at the baptist church parking lot. closest schools to kennard (zip 47351) knightstown high school 1 panther trail, knightstown, in 46148 mileage from 100 n main st: ~8 miles official website https://www.cabeard.k12.in.us/ rankings https://www.niche.com/k12/knightstown-high-school-knightstown-in/ shenandoah high school 1100 w 600 n, middletown, in 47356 mileage from 100 n main st: ~12 miles official website https://www.shenandoah.k12.in.us/ rankings https://www.niche.com/k12/shenandoah-high-school-middletown-in/ eastern hancock high school 1 trojan lane, charlottesville, in 46117 mileage from 100 n main st: ~15 miles official website https://www.eastern.k12.in.us/ rankings https://www.niche.com/k12/d/eastern-hancock-county-community-school-corporation-in new castle high school 1000 n 15th st, new castle, in 47362 mileage from 100 n main st: ~10 miles official website https://www.nccsc.k12.in.us/ rankings https://www.niche.com/k12/new-castle-high-school-new-castle-in/ notable sports accomplishments knightstown high school (~8 miles) boys basketball: sectional champions 2018, 2022 football: regional champions 2019 shenandoah high school (~12 miles) girls basketball: state champions 2003, 2005 boys basketball: sectional champions multiple years including 2004, 2006 eastern hancock high school (~15 miles) boys basketball: sectional champions 2017, 2021 new castle high school (~10 miles) boys basketball: state champions 1985, 1986 (steve alford era) memorable local alumni steve alford new castle high school (class of 1983) achievements: indiana mr. basketball 1983 • ncaa national champion 1987 (indiana hoosiers) • nba player (dallas mavericks, golden state warriors) • head coach at ucla and new mexico. local importance: led new castle to back-to-back state championships in the mid-1980s. one of indiana's most iconic high school basketball legends and a proud new castle graduate who put henry county on the national map. kent benson new castle high school achievements: nba player (milwaukee bucks, detroit pistons) • ncaa champion with indiana in 1976 • 3-time all-big ten selection. local importance: another new castle basketball star who helped bring statewide recognition to the trojans program in the 1970s.

content: minutes & agendas - town of kennard watch meetings on youtube youtube channel 2026 minutes & agendas 2026 minutes 2026 agendas 2025 minutes & agendas 2025 minutes 2025 agendas meeting schedule second wednesday of every month at 7:00 pm at town hall. understanding agendas and minutes agendas outline the topics planned for discussion at each meeting. agendas are available before the meetings. they are often not digitally uploaded prior to the meeting. attendees can have a copy at the meetings. minutes provide the official record of what was discussed and decided during the meeting. viewing documents all minutes and agendas are posted on this page as soon as they are available.

title: Town Board - Town of Kennard
snippet: Town Board - Town of Kennard Steve Worsham President since 2020 Steve is passionate about streets and utility infrastructure improvements and enhancing services for the entire community. Jason Groce Member & Former President Has held office since 2015. Jason is focused on creatin
content: town board - town of kennard steve worsham president since 2020 steve is passionate about streets and utility infrastructure improvements and enhancing services for the entire community. jason groce member & former president has held office since 2015. jason is focused on creating policies that benefit the citizens and increasing the town’s technology infrastructure. holly lee member served since 2017. holly is deeply dedicated to giving and helping citizens. she serves on the volunteer fire department and donates countless community hours for greensboro township while also working her full-time job. meeting schedule second wednesday of every month at 7:00 pm at town hall. role of the town board the board sets policy, approves budgets, and represents the citizens of kennard. public participation citizens are encouraged to address the board during the public comment period at each meeting. board members are to be addressed only during public meetings.
